steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize the RELATIVE sizes of the two capitals' populations — i.e., directly compare Hanoi's population with Ulaanbaatar's.

steps.title: Look for explicit comparison
steps.content: Choice C uses the comparative word "larger": "Hanoi (population 7,781,631) is larger than Ulaanbaatar (population 907,802)." That structure foregrounds the size difference rather than just listing numbers.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Ulaanbaatar... 907,802... Hanoi... 7,781,631': lists the two populations without comparing them; reader must do the work. ✗
  • Choice B — '8.14 percent... 31.98 percent': compares each city's share of its country, which is the wrong dimension (not relative sizes of the capitals). ✗
  • Choice C — 'Hanoi... is larger than Ulaanbaatar': explicit comparative emphasizes the relative sizes. ✓
  • Choice D — '907,802... and 7,781,631... respectively': pairs cities with numbers but, like A, omits any comparative language. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
